text: A UIUC junior in engineering offers personalized tutoring in calculus and linear algebra for students taking introductory and intermediate math courses. Sessions are designed around the student's syllabus, homework set, and exam schedule, with extra practice on topics such as derivatives, integration techniques, vector spaces, matrices, determinants, and eigenvalues. The tutor focuses on explaining why methods work instead of only showing formulas, so students can solve unfamiliar problems independently. Meetings can be held at Grainger, the Main Library, or over Zoom, and review packets can be created before midterms. This service is especially useful for students who want structured weekly support, last-minute exam preparation, or patient help working through difficult lecture material. A UIUC computer science student provides one-on-one programming tutoring for beginners learning Python or Java. The service helps students understand syntax, debugging, loops, functions, arrays, object-oriented programming, and common assignment patterns used in introductory CS courses. Sessions typically include a short concept review, guided coding practice, and time for debugging class projects or homework submissions. The tutor can also explain how to read compiler errors, write cleaner code, and prepare for coding quizzes and technical interviews. Meetings are available online or in campus study spaces, and students can book single sessions or recurring weekly help. This listing is ideal for anyone who feels stuck in class and wants practical explanations with step-by-step examples.

A pre-med student offers tutoring for general chemistry, organic chemistry foundations, and introductory biology courses at UIUC. Sessions focus on lecture review, reaction patterns, problem solving, vocabulary retention, and test-taking strategies for science-heavy exams. Students can bring lecture slides, worksheets, and practice tests, and the tutor will help organize the material into manageable study goals. Support is available for understanding mechanisms, cellular processes, lab concepts, and multiple-choice reasoning. The tutor emphasizes consistency by creating weekly study plans that fit around classes and club commitments. Appointments can be scheduled before major exams or used throughout the semester. This service works well for students who want accountability, clearer explanations, and extra confidence before quizzes and midterms.

A student photographer offers graduation photo sessions around popular UIUC landmarks including the Quad, Alma Mater, Foellinger Auditorium, and the Main Library steps. Each session includes pose guidance, candid shots, solo portraits, and small group photos with friends or family. Clients can bring multiple outfits, cap and gown accessories, champagne props, or cultural stoles to customize the shoot. Edited high-resolution images are delivered in an online gallery within seven days, with color correction and light retouching included. The photographer aims to capture both polished portraits and natural celebratory moments that feel personal to the graduate's UIUC experience. This service is ideal for seniors who want professional-looking photos without booking a large studio package.

A UIUC student photographer provides event coverage for registered student organizations, birthday celebrations, cultural performances, and campus socials. The service includes candid coverage, posed group photos, speaker highlights, and crowd atmosphere images that organizers can later use for recap posts, recruitment, and memory books. The photographer is comfortable working in indoor and low-light environments and brings equipment suited for fast-moving events. After the event, clients receive a curated digital album with edited images and clear file organization for easy sharing. Pricing depends on event duration and expected photo volume, and longer bookings can include same-week turnaround for promotional needs. This listing is a good fit for clubs and student groups that want reliable visual coverage without hiring a commercial team.

A student hairstylist offers hair styling appointments for formals, graduation shoots, birthdays, and other special occasions. A UIUC student offers dependable pet sitting for cats, dogs, rabbits, and other small companion animals while owners are traveling, studying for exams, or managing a busy week. A UIUC student with internship recruiting experience offers resume reviews, LinkedIn feedback, and interview preparation for students applying to internships, campus jobs, and entry-level roles. Clients may send documents in advance for written comments before the live session begins. This service is ideal for students who want practical application advice from someone recently familiar with the recruiting process.

A UIUC student offers moving help during move-in, move-out, sublease changes, and apartment furniture pickups. Support can include carrying boxes, disassembling small furniture, organizing loading order, and helping transport items between buildings when arranged in advance. The service is especially popular at the beginning and end of semesters, when elevators, parking, and scheduling can become stressful. Clients should share the building location, number of stairs, and item types before booking so the workload can be estimated fairly. Hourly rates are available, and two-person help may be coordinated for heavier jobs depending on timing. This listing is ideal for students without a car or those who need an extra set of hands to make a demanding move more manageable.
